define.description: Binds a name to a value or defines a procedure with parameters and body expressions in the current environment, serving as the primary definition form in Scheme programs.

lambda.description: Creates an anonymous procedure (closure) that captures its lexical environment, accepting the specified formal parameters and evaluating the body expressions when called.

let.description: Introduces local bindings where all initial value expressions are evaluated in the outer scope before any bindings take effect, then evaluates the body in the extended environment.

let*.description: Sequential binding form where each variable binding is visible to subsequent binding expressions, allowing later bindings to reference earlier ones in the same let block.

letrec.description: Recursive binding form where all variables are in scope for all expressions, enabling mutually recursive procedure definitions within a local binding block.

cond.description: Multi-way conditional expression that evaluates test clauses in order and returns the value of the expression sequence for the first true test, with an optional else clause.

car.description: Returns the first element (head) of a pair or the first element of a non-empty list, raising an error if applied to an empty list or non-pair value.

cdr.description: Returns the second element (tail) of a pair, or the rest of a list after removing the first element, serving as the fundamental list decomposition operation.

cons.description: Constructs a new pair from two values; when the second argument is a list, produces a new list with the first argument prepended as the head element.

map.description: Applies a procedure element-wise to corresponding elements of one or more lists and returns a new list of the results, preserving the order of elements.

fold-left.description: Reduces a list from left to right by applying a binary procedure to an accumulator (starting with init) and each element, returning the final accumulated value.

call/cc.description: Captures the current continuation as a first-class escape procedure and passes it to the given procedure, enabling non-local exits, coroutines, and advanced control flow patterns.

syntax-rules.description: Defines a hygienic macro transformer using pattern matching rules, where each pattern is matched against use-site forms and the corresponding template generates the expansion.

values.description: Returns multiple values from a procedure, which can be received by call-with-values or let-values, enabling functions to produce more than one result efficiently.

apply.description: Calls a procedure with arguments taken from a list, optionally preceded by individual arguments, effectively spreading the list elements as positional parameters.

for-each.description: Applies a procedure to corresponding elements of one or more lists in order purely for side effects, guaranteed to process elements sequentially unlike map.

dynamic-wind.description: Executes thunk between before and after procedures, guaranteeing that before runs on entry and after on exit even when continuations transfer control in or out.
